methods.description: The process of retrieving sediment cores from specific locations in Puck Bay using different coring devices during research cruises.

steps.description: Cores ZP3/0414 was retrived by means of a Niemistö corer during a cruise of ORP "Heweliusz" in April 2014.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve sediment core using Niemistö corer

steps.description: Rest of the cores, ZP1/0518 and ZP2/0518, were collected with a Rumohr-Lot corer from r/v "Oceanograf" in May 2018.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve sediment cores using Rumohr-Lot corer

steps.description: Sediments with undisturbed structure were obtained from the water depth of 47 to 65 m b.s.l. along the outer part of Hel Peninsula (table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Record core collection location and depth metadata
inputs: location description (outer part of Hel Peninsula)

methods.method_name: Sediment Sample Preparation for Diatom Analysis
methods.description: Standard laboratory procedure to chemically treat sediment samples to isolate diatoms and prepare permanent slides for microscopic analysis.

steps.description: Sediment samples were taken in 1 -5 cm intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample sediment core at specified intervals

steps.description: In order to prepare a permanent slide from each sample, they were treated according to standard method [18]. The samples were treated with 10 % HCl to remove calcium carbonate
steps.searchable_summary: Treat sediment samples with HCl to remove calcium carbonate

steps.description: and washed several times with distilled water.
The organic matter was digested using 30% H2O2,
after which mineral matter was removed by decantation.
Permanent diatom slides were mounted in Naphrax® (refractive index nD=1.73).
steps.searchable_summary: Mount samples onto slides using Naphrax

methods.description: Microscopic examination of prepared slides to identify, count, and quantify diatom valves according to a standard procedure.

steps.description: Qualitative diatom analysis was carried out with Nikon ECLIPSE 400 microscope at a magnification of × 1000, using an oil immersion objective.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform qualitative diatom analysis using microscopy
outputs: initial list of observed diatom taxa

steps.description: Over 500 valves of diatoms were counted in each sample according to Schrader and Gersonde [19] procedure
outputs: diatom valve counts per sample (>= 500 valves)

steps.description: and percentage content of all noted taxa was estimated.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate percentage content of each diatom taxon
outputs: percentage abundance of each diatom taxon per sample

methods.description: Classification of identified diatom taxa into ecological groups based on various systems (biotype, halobian, trophic, saprobic) and calculation of the percentage abundance of each group per sample.

steps.description: The diatoms identified were classified with respect to their ecological preferences.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify identified diatoms by ecological preference

steps.description: The diatoms were divided into groups according to their biotype requirements; planktic and benthic groups were distinguished [20].
On the basis of Kolbe's [21] halobian system they were grouped into: euhalobous (marine species), mesohalobous (brackish-water), oligohalobous (freshwater) halophilous and indifferent.
steps.searchable_summary: Group diatoms by halobian system (salinity preference)
outputs: diatom taxa grouped by halobian system

steps.description: Moreover, trophic system with eutraphentic, eu-mesotraphentic, mesotraphentic, oligotraphentic and dystraphentic taxa [22] was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Group diatoms by trophic system (nutrient preference)
outputs: diatom taxa grouped by trophic system

steps.description: Finally, the diatom flora were divided according to saprobic preferences [23] and polisaprobous, α-mesosaprobous, mesosaprobous, oligosaprobous, xenosaprobous diatoms were distinguished in each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Group diatoms by saprobic preferences (organic pollution tolerance)
outputs: diatom taxa grouped by saprobic preferences

steps.description: Next, the percentage content of all ecological groups were counted in the cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate percentage content of each ecological group per sample
inputs: percentage abundance of each diatom taxon per sample
diatom taxa grouped by ecological categories
outputs: percentage abundance of each ecological group per sample

steps.description: The taxonomy and autecology was based on Omnidia 6.0.8 software.
steps.searchable_summary: Use Omnidia software for taxonomy and autecology
